subject: Public Health
title: Malaria Through the Ages
description: One-minute skill: Malaria Through the Ages.

hook.text: Malaria may have killed more humans throughout history than any other single disease.

buildup.text: Ancient Egyptians, Greeks, and Romans all described cyclical fevers matching malaria symptoms.

discovery.text: The name means 'bad air'—for centuries people blamed swamps, not knowing mosquitoes carried it.

twist.text: Ronald Ross proved in 1897 that mosquitoes transmit malaria—winning the Nobel Prize.

climax.text: Despite a century of effort, malaria still kills over 600,000 people per year, mostly African children.

punchline.text: Humanity's oldest killer is still at work.

quiz.question: Who proved that mosquitoes transmit malaria?
